body.question_1: Reflecting on the full academic year, how would you rate your overall educational experience at this university?
body.answer_1: Reflecting on the full academic year in the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning program at the School of Information Technology, I can say with confidence that this has been an commendable and transformative educational experience that has exceeded my expectations in most meaningful ways. The combination of rigorous coursework, dedicated faculty, strong peer community, and access to industry connections has equipped me with both the technical competencies and professional mindset needed to succeed in my chosen field. I have grown significantly as a learner and feel genuinely prepared for the next stage of my academic or professional journey. I commend the institution for maintaining high standards throughout and look forward to continuing my studies here with great enthusiasm.

body.question_2: How effectively do you believe this year's coursework has prepared you for your future career or further academic studies?
body.answer_2: Career preparation services at the School of Information Technology, offered through the Career Center, are reasonable and meet some student needs while falling short in others. Basic resume guidance and periodic career fairs provide a foundation, but more intensive and personalized support would greatly benefit students planning to enter a competitive and rapidly evolving job market. The internship program has room for significant growth — expanding the employer partner network, improving placement matching, and providing structured mentorship during internship periods would make it substantially more valuable. Regular industry panels and alumni engagement events would also meaningfully bridge the gap between academic preparation and professional workplace expectations.

body.question_3: How would you assess the progression of your academic skills and knowledge throughout this academic year?
body.answer_3: The Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning curriculum at the School of Information Technology covers the essential theoretical foundations competently and provides a reasonable structure for academic progression throughout the degree. Core modules are satisfactory in their depth and breadth, covering key concepts that form the basis of professional practice in this field. However, the elective offerings could be significantly expanded to reflect emerging trends and specialized career pathways that students are increasingly interested in. Greater integration of industry collaboration — such as guest lectures, live projects, and sponsored capstone work — would substantially strengthen the curriculum's practical relevance and better prepare graduates for competitive employment.

body.question_4: How satisfied are you with the extracurricular activities, student clubs, and personal development opportunities available?
body.answer_4: The extracurricular opportunities available at the School of Information Technology, coordinated by the Student Affairs Office, are superb and represent a genuinely important complement to the formal academic curriculum. A wide and diverse range of student clubs, cultural societies, sports teams, and volunteer programs are actively supported and well-resourced by the university, enabling students to develop leadership skills, build social connections, and pursue interests outside their academic programs. Events are well-promoted, regularly scheduled, and inclusive in their design, welcoming students from all backgrounds and programs to participate equally. I commend the institution's recognition that holistic student development extends beyond classroom performance and into every dimension of campus life.

body.question_5: How well has the university's Career Center prepared you for internship opportunities and graduate employment?
body.answer_5: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Information Technology has been insufficient and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I recommend the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_6: How would you rate the university's financial services, including tuition fee management and scholarship support?
body.answer_6: The financial services provided by the Finance Department at the School of Information Technology have been excellent this semester, and I genuinely recognize the clarity, transparency, and efficiency with which they are managed. Fee statements are issued promptly and accurately, with clear breakdowns of tuition, facility charges, and any applicable scholarship deductions that make it easy to verify and plan payments. Payment options are flexible and well-communicated, including installment plans that meaningfully reduce financial pressure on students and families. Scholarship disbursements are processed on time and confirmed through official notifications, removing uncertainty around critical financial support. The finance office staff are professional, patient, and genuinely helpful when navigating complex financial queries.

body.question_7: How would you evaluate the IT infrastructure and digital services provided by the university over the course of the year?
body.answer_7: The IT infrastructure at the School of Information Technology, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a satisfactory level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_8: How satisfied are you with the library resources — both physical and digital — that were available throughout the academic year?
body.answer_8: The learning materials provided for several of my Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning courses at the School of Information Technology are concerning and fall short of what students need to succeed academically. Required textbooks are outdated and frequently out of stock in both the campus bookstore and the library, forcing students to source materials independently at considerable expense and inconvenience. The Learning Management System is inconsistently used by lecturers — some post materials days after class, and some modules have no digital resources whatsoever. I strongly urge the Library Services and IT Support Department to collaborate on ensuring all course resources are accessible digitally, current, and available before the semester commences.

body.question_9: How effectively did the university communicate important information, deadlines, and updates throughout the academic year?
body.answer_9: Academic administrative processes at the School of Information Technology are reasonable in their execution, meeting basic requirements but leaving clear room for improvement in both speed and user-friendliness. The online student portal functions adequately for routine tasks such as registration and fee payment, but navigating more complex requests — such as grade appeals or course exemptions — remains unnecessarily cumbersome. Communication timelines are inconsistent, with some requests handled promptly and others taking weeks without updates or acknowledgment. Streamlining administrative workflows, improving the student portal's UX, and setting clear service-level response standards would meaningfully reduce administrative friction for the entire student body.

body.question_10: What are your key recommendations for improving the overall student experience in the coming academic year?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning student experience at the School of Information Technology center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only satisfactory outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
